5.description: Average paths of the economy around systemic and nonsystemic crises. Crises events happen att=0$t=0$. The model is simulated for 500,000 periods. Series in Panels A, C, D are in percentage deviations of respective variables from their long-run averages. Series in Panel B are in standard deviations from the long-run mean. Series in Panels E and F are in percentages. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Behavior of credit (Panel A), output (Panel B), and the output-to-credit ratio (Panel C) around systemic financial crises in the data and in the model. All series are HP-filtered with a smoothing parameter of 6.25 and are expressed in percentage deviations from their HP-trends. Shaded areas show two-standard-deviation bands. Systemic crises are defined as in Romer and Romer (2017). Credit and output data come from Jorda, Schularick, and Taylor (2016). Model series are based on 500,000 simulations. Credit in the model is defined as the amount of assets invested in the risky technology,A1גˆ’־¼ֲ¯1גˆ’־¼$A\frac{1-\bar{\mu }}{1-\mu }$. Crises happen att=0$t=0$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Measures of aggregate bank portfolio overlap (Panel A), bank portfolio diversification (Panel B), and correlation across bank profitabilities (Panel C) in the model and in the data. The shaded area marks the Great Recession according to the NBER timing. For Panels A and B, the measures are constructed using syndicated loan portfolios from DealScan. Loan portfolios are constructed at the industry (one- and two-digit SIC) and state levels. In the model, portfolio overlap is1גˆ’(־±גˆ’(1גˆ’־±)/(Nגˆ’1))$1-(\alpha -(1-\alpha )/(N-1))$and portfolio diversification is1גˆ’־±2גˆ’(1גˆ’־±)2/(Nגˆ’1)$1-\alpha ^{2}-(1-\alpha )^{2}/(N-1)$. For Panel C, the measure is the asset-weighted average correlation of bank-specific returns on equity with the aggregate return on equity. Correlations are computed using a 12-quarter rolling window. The data come from the FR Y-9C reports. In the model, profitability cross-correlation is an average pairwise correlation of־±xi+1גˆ’־±Nגˆ’1גˆ‘jג‰ ixj$\alpha x_{i}+\frac{1-\alpha }{N-1}\sum _{j\protect \ne i}x_{j}$across investing banks (computed numerically). For all panels, model series are average paths of corresponding variables around systemic financial crises. They are based on 500,000 simulations. Series in Panels A and B are normalized to one at the beginning of the sample. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Average paths of the benchmark (DE) and the constrained efficient (SB) economies around systemic crises (t= 0). The models are simulated for 500,000 periods. Series in Panels A and C are in percentage deviations of the respective variables from their long-run averages. Series in Panel B are in standard deviations from the long-run mean. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
